We have this in larger, we always print the detail on the fine print head even with the larger version.

Just finished a full colour 432 Park Avenue print and it's come out sharp.

Ice blue windows with a clean white structure really suit this one — simple design but it stands tall on display. The grid pattern turned out beautifully.

One of London's most recognisable skylines, now in miniature.

Our Walkie Talkie Building (20 Fenchurch Street) 3D printed model captures the distinctive curved profile that makes it stand out against the city skyline.

China Zun (CITIC Tower) is a building you almost never see offered as a physical model — and I haven't seen it sold elsewhere in this format.

This is a 3D-printed scale model of Beijing's tallest skyscraper.

The Ryugyong Hotel is brutalism at its most unapologetic.
No curves, no charm, no attempt to be friendly — just mass, symmetry, and intent.

Rising 330 metres over Pyongyang, it was designed to dominate.

Just finished listing the World Trade Center Twin Towers as a matched 3D-printed pair.

These are scaled to roof height for consistency, with the North Tower antenna included on top. Each set is printed to order.

Jeddah Tower has a presence that's hard to ignore — even as a model.

I've just added this 3D-printed Jeddah Tower to the shop, scaled directly from the real 1,000 m design. Clean lines, ultra-slender.

Bring New York's skyline into your home.

This full-colour 3D-printed model of the One World Trade Center captures every sharp line and striking angle of the iconic tower.

Just finished this 1:1000 scale model of Wrigley Field. The whole stadium is printed in light grey, with the field in green and the infield in light brown, so it has a clean architectural look but still feels alive.
A few Chicago models finished up — really happy with how the bronze stands out against the black. Added a new baseball stand too, and I'm genuinely impressed with how it turned out.

Just finished this full-colour 3D print of the Leadenhall Building (aka the Cheesegrater) — and it turned out beautifully.

At 1:1000 scale, it stands 22.5cm tall and is packed with detail.
